transcript.clean_text: welcome to highlights of stage six of La boîte estefania right here on GC and racing yesterday we had two races in one firstly at the front we wrangled Madrazo who'd been yo-yoing off the front and off the back of the lead group eventually came good just at the right time to take one of the most memorable stage victories for some time behind Lopez proved strongest of the GC contenders coming to the line 12 seconds clear of Robert and Valverde taking the red jersey back 14 seconds clear of Rutledge today could also be described as a summit finish although not nearly as hard as yesterday's a shade under 200 kilometers there were four climbs - at the start and two near the finish perfect for another breakaway success so the long time though for that breakaway to form such was the interest in getting into it in fact it took over 50 kilometers of flight acting before eleven riders broke clear just after the midway point in the stage there was a huge crash in the bunch before TV coverage began it led to the withdrawal of Victor della part as well as EF education first euro Rigoberta Iran and Hugh Karthi a few days later Nico wrote it also abandoned though the injuries sustained from the very crash it was a quality group out the front Oliveira good on turns polyansky van garderen MRI groom a de la Cruz kissing Brambilla and hazus Harada dela Cruz is the best place on the GC at 4 minutes 35 on the placement climb of the day grrah may decide it was time to light the blue touch paper he was the first of the break to make a move and quickly established a rather healthy looking time gap it was up to almost 30 seconds as he went through the 25k to go marker behind there were yet more woes for EF education first this time it was TJ bang garden crashing out of the breakaway but thankfully not out of the race things can only get better as they say behind Gamay they'd been a flurry of attacks but the one that stuck was this one from Oliveira of Moby star he quickly bridged up to Gamay leaving us two out front with 20 k's to go all was not lost so for those behind who continued to roll through enough and work well together pegging the gap and making sure it didn't get dangerously out of control by this point dela Cruz with the virtual race leader on Road with a star not setting a blistering pace behind on to the final climb the leaders advance had grown to over 40 seconds but it wasn't long before the action kicked off with the rest of the group Dillon churns biding his time and sent some weakness from dela Cruz going on the attack bridging across to the small group in front and going straight past them he had team confidences hisses Harada glued to his will they'd go straight past groom a who by this point was paying for his efforts and back up to the wheel of Nelson Oliveira however the motel rider also wasn't able to stick with the pace leaving us with two out front turns riding his heart out with the strong possibility of taking the lead as red jersey Harada happy to sit on the wheel and concentrate on the stage win although as you can see from the grimaces on his face just sticking on the wheel was not an easy effort it's one of those weird idiosyncrasies of sight commits to rise can both come out of a situation like this pleased with the outcome and that was exactly the case today Harada had enough left to sprint to the line to take his first Grand Tour stage win whilst terms could be contend to rise hard as he could to the line in second place but almost guaranteed the overall race lead in the main Bunch there wasn't really much in the way of action until the youthful exuberance of Taddy pocket are launched into action he put her searing acceleration and quickly distance the rest of the group wearing the white jersey on behalf of lopez pocket art is the youngest rider in this race just 20 years of age but he certainly doesn't look out of his depth does he he'd eventually crossed a line with quite a slim advantage over PA Latour and the rest behind but I for one am very excited to see what this young man can do over the remaining two weeks the state don't all about this man Hayes's Harada the Spanish rider from Team coffee is that is how much it means to win a stayed in your home Grand Tour absolutely brilliant to see it advantage seven seconds in the end over Dillon Tunes great riding good on as well in third place for ADA two are Robert hare sync on the same time in fourth what a season this mans having though still unchanged a few weeks ago on his first ever stage at the Tour de France and now he was leader of the world as Spanier 38 seconds clear of de la Cruz one minute clear of the full race leader Miguel Angle Lopez tomorrow we have yet another summit finish this time on the master La Coste the Clym is short but brutal 12.3% the average kradin for the last four days with a maximum of 22 percent my prediction tally poacher I'll see you again tomorrow
